parts.text:
Lee AGENTS.md, PRD.md, architecture.md y data_model.md
para ponerte en contexto del proyecto.

Modo Plan. Issue #25: Correcciones visuales menores.

CORRECCIÓN 1 - Unidad de capacidad incorrecta:
En templates/store_detail.html la capacidad del Store
se muestra con unidades m² cuando debe ser m³.
Cambiar m² por m³ en todos los lugares donde aparezca.

CORRECCIÓN 2 - País duplicado en vista Almacenes:
En templates/stores.html el código de país aparece
duplicado en cada fila: se muestra la bandera y el
código de texto por separado, resultando en dos
valores (ej. "ES" y "ES"). Mostrar solo una vez:
bandera + código en una sola celda sin repetición.

CORRECCIÓN 3 - Símbolo # en valores de tabla Productos:
En templates/products.html las celdas de la columna
de ubicaciones muestran "8 # Ubicaciones" en lugar
de solo el número "8". Eliminar el símbolo # y el
texto "Ubicaciones" del valor de cada celda, dejando
solo el número.

CORRECCIÓN 4 - Representación 3D: tamaño de productos
y distribución:
En static/store_3d.js los objetos 3D que representan
los productos dentro de las estanterías son demasiado
pequeños. Aumentar su tamaño para que sean más visibles
y aprovechen mejor el espacio disponible en cada
estantería. Además, la distribución de las estanterías
en el eje Z no es simétrica: hay más espacio en un lado
que en otro. Corregir para que la distribución sea
centrada y simétrica respecto al centro de la escena.

CORRECCIÓN 5 - Overlay 3D: limitar a 4 productos con
scroll:
En el overlay que aparece al hacer hover sobre una
estantería en la escena Three.js, mostrar un máximo
de 4 productos visibles a la vez. Si hay más de 4
productos en esa estantería, mostrar una barra
deslizante lateral (scrollbar CSS) para ver el resto.
Implementar con CSS puro usando overflow-y: auto y
max-height equivalente a 4 productos, sin JS adicional
y sin interferir con los controles OrbitControls de
la escena 3D. Asegurarse de que el scroll del overlay
no propague el evento al zoom de la escena usando
stopPropagation en el evento wheel del overlay.

Solo elabora el plan. No implementes nada todavía.

Lee AGENTS.md, PRD.md, architecture.md y data_model.md
para ponerte en contexto del proyecto.

Modo Plan. Issue #25: Correcciones visuales menores.

CORRECCIÓN 1 - Unidad de capacidad incorrecta:
En templates/store_detail.html la capacidad del Store
se muestra con unidades m² cuando debe ser m³.
Cambiar m² por m³ en todos los lugares donde aparezca.

CORRECCIÓN 2 - País duplicado en vista Almacenes:
En templates/stores.html el código de país aparece
duplicado en cada fila: se muestra la bandera y el
código de texto por separado, resultando en dos
valores (ej. "ES" y "ES"). Mostrar solo una vez:
bandera + código en una sola celda sin repetición.

CORRECCIÓN 3 - Símbolo # en valores de tabla Productos:
En templates/products.html las celdas de la columna
de ubicaciones muestran "8 # Ubicaciones" en lugar
de solo el número "8". Eliminar el símbolo # y el
texto "Ubicaciones" del valor de cada celda, dejando
solo el número.

CORRECCIÓN 4 - Representación 3D: tamaño de productos
y distribución:
En static/store_3d.js los objetos 3D que representan
los productos dentro de las estanterías son demasiado
pequeños. Aumentar su tamaño para que sean más visibles
y aprovechen mejor el espacio disponible en cada
estantería. Además, la distribución de las estanterías
en el eje Z no es simétrica: hay más espacio en un lado
que en otro. Corregir para que la distribución sea
centrada y simétrica respecto al centro de la escena.

CORRECCIÓN 5 - Overlay 3D: limitar a 4 productos con
scroll:
En el overlay que aparece al hacer hover sobre una
estantería en la escena Three.js, mostrar un máximo
de 4 productos visibles a la vez. Si hay más de 4
productos en esa estantería, mostrar una barra
deslizante lateral (scrollbar CSS) para ver el resto.
Implementar con CSS puro usando overflow-y: auto y
max-height equivalente a 4 productos, sin JS adicional
y sin interferir con los controles OrbitControls de
la escena 3D. Asegurarse de que el scroll del overlay
no propague el evento al zoom de la escena usando
stopPropagation en el evento wheel del overlay.

Solo elabora el plan. No implementes nada todavía.

<skills>
Here is a list of skills that contain domain specific knowledge on a variety of topics.
Each skill comes with a description of the topic and a file path that contains the detailed instructions.
When a user asks you to perform a task that falls within the domain of a skill, use the #tool:readFile tool to acquire the full instructions from the file URI.
<skill>
<name>agent-customization</name>
<description>**WORKFLOW SKILL** — Create, update, review, fix, or debug VS Code agent customization files (.instructions.md, .prompt.md, .agent.md, SKILL.md, copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md). USE FOR: saving coding preferences; troubleshooting why instructions/skills/agents are ignored or not invoked; configuring applyTo patterns; defining tool restrictions; creating custom agent modes or specialized workflows; packaging domain knowledge; fixing YAML frontmatter syntax. DO NOT USE FOR: general coding questions (use default agent); runtime debugging or error diagnosis; MCP server configuration (use MCP docs directly); VS Code extension development. INVOKES: file system tools (read/write customization files), ask-questions tool (interview user for requirements), subagents for codebase exploration. FOR SINGLE OPERATIONS: For quick YAML frontmatter fixes or creating a single file from a known pattern, edit the file directly — no skill needed.</description>
<file>copilot-skill:/agent-customization/SKILL.md</file>
</skill>
</skills>


<agents>
Here is a list of agents that can be used when running a subagent.
Each agent has optionally a description with the agent's purpose and expertise. When asked to run a subagent, choose the most appropriate agent from this list.
Use the #tool:runSubagent tool with the agent name to run the subagent.
<agent>
<name>Explore</name>
<description>Fast read-only codebase exploration and Q&A subagent. Prefer over manually chaining multiple search and file-reading operations to avoid cluttering the main conversation. Safe to call in parallel. Specify thoroughness: quick, medium, or thorough.</description>
<argumentHint>Describe WHAT you're looking for and desired thoroughness (quick/medium/thorough)</argumentHint>
</agent>
</agents>

toolSpecificData.prompt: Read and summarize AGENTS.md, PRD.md, architecture.md, and data_model.md in the workspace. Focus on constraints, conventions, and anything relevant to UI/3D/store capacity units/country display/products table/3D overlay behavior. Thoroughness: medium. Do NOT modify files. Return concise bullets and mention exact file paths examined.
toolSpecificData.result:
## Summary

### Files Examined
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)
- [PRD.md](PRD.md)
- [architecture.md](architecture.md)
- [data_model.md](data_model.md)

---

### **CONSTRAINTS**

**Model & Data Coherence:**
- `shelfCount ≤ stockCount` (always)
- `stockCount` = sum of all `shelfCount` for (Product, Store)
- `numberOfItems` = sum of `shelfCount` values for that Shelf (coherence validation)
- **Each Shelf MUST contain MINIMUM 4 distinct Products**
- Orion 4.1.0: All numeric types use `"type": "Number"` (not `"Integer"`)
- Orion 4.1.0: String values **ASCII only** (no accents: "Maria" not "María", "Almacen" not "Almacén")
- Orion 4.1.0: Image URLs without query params (`no ?w=400`)

**Shelf Capacity (Physical):**
- Max capacity per Shelf: **32 units** (grid layout 4×4×2)
- `maxCapacity` must be 0 < maxCapacity ≤ 32
- Cannot reduce `maxCapacity` below current occupation
- Occupation = sum of all `shelfCount` on that Shelf
- Validation: `occupation_shelf + new_units ≤ maxCapacity_shelf`

**Store & Location:**
- `countryCode`: exactly 2 characters [A-Za-z] (ISO 2-char format: ES, FR, IT)
- `location`: GeoJSON Point [lon, lat] valid WGS84
- `temperature`, `relativeHumidity`, `tweets`: **READ-ONLY** (from external providers, no client modification)

**InventoryItem:**
- `shelfCount ≤ 0`: Button "Comprar" disabled
- Stock change (buy): Direct PATCH from client to Orion (no Flask intermediation)
- **Low stock trigger**: `shelfCount < 3` → fires `low_stock` notification

**Webhooks (Critical):**
- Use `http://host.docker.internal:5000/notify/...` (**NOT** `localhost`)

**NGSIv2 Format (Rigid):**
- All attributes: `{"type": "Text|Number|etc", "value": ...}`
- Pattern matching: Use `"idPattern": ".*"` (not `"isPattern": true`)

---

### **CONVENTIONS**

**Identifiers (Orion URN):**
- Format: `urn:ngsi-ld:<EntityType>:<UniqueIdentifier>`
- Case: kebab-case for identifiers (e.g., `urn:ngsi-ld:Store:madrid-centro`)

**Routes (Flask):**
- Pattern: `/<Entity>/<action>` or `/<Entity>/<id>/<action>`
- HTTP methods: standard REST (GET, POST, PATCH)

**Variables:**
- Python: 4-space indent, 100-char max line, snake_case
- JavaScript: camelCase variables, UPPER_CASE constants, 2-space indent, `const` preferred, arrow functions
- CSS: kebab-case with prefixes, 2-space indent, CSS custom properties (`--variable-name`)
- HTML/Jinja2: 2-space indent, semantic tags, `data-i18n` for visible text, `data-*` for JS hooks

**Templates:**
- Pattern: `<Entity>-<action>.html` or `<Entity>.html`

**Documents:**
- Update after each feature/fix merge: `PRD.md`, `architecture.md`, `data_model.md`

---

### **UI / 3D / STORE CAPACITY / COUNTRY / PRODUCTS TABLE / 3D OVERLAY**

**Store Detail - View Layout:**
- Photo, sensors (temperature/humidity with color-coded indicators)
- **Leaflet.js map** with store markers and environmental tooltips
- **Three.js 3D traversal** with floor plan + interactive shelves
- Inventory table grouped by shelf with progress bars
- Tweets panel
- Real-time notifications panel
- Environmental data from external providers

**3D Traversal (Issue #22):**
- **Grid Layout**: 4×4×2 (columns × levels × depth) = max 32 units per shelf
- **Representation**: Each `shelfCount` unit = independent 3D cube
- **Texture Pipeline**:
  - Initial: `MeshStandardMaterial` with `Product.color` (hex #RRGGBB)
  - On texture load: assign `material.map`, force `material.color = #FFFFFF` (anti-tinting), enable `needsUpdate`
  - On texture error: keep color fallback (no degradation)
- **Interaction**:
  - OrbitControls (rotate, zoom/dolly, pan)
  - Hover: shelf highlight
  - Click/tap: camera centers on shelf with smooth animation
- **Overlay (2D per shelf)**:
  - Product name + shelfCount + stockCount per product
  - Dynamic centering from real shelf positions
  - Pan limits in X/Z to prevent scene loss
  - Depth-based overlay prioritization
  - **Strict clipping**: overlays contained within 3D container (no navbar invasion)
  - Resolved via `products_dict` (no extra requests)

**Products Table:**
- Columns: image, name, price, size, color, stock (total), quantity_on_shelf
- Real-time price updates via Socket.IO (`price_change` event)
- "Comprar" button per InventoryItem
  - Disabled if `shelfCount ≤ 0`
  - Direct PATCH to Orion (no reload)

**Store List (Country Display):**
- Columns/fields: photo, name, **country** (from `countryCode`), temperature (color-coded), humidity (color-coded)
- Temperature/humidity indicators use color thresholds for visual feedback

**Data Query Pattern (Critical - Issue #9):**
- Always include **critical attributes** in `include_attrs`, not just filters:
  - Example: `"name,temperature,relativeHumidity,tweets"` (include `name` even if filtering other attrs)
  - Omitting `name` causes `AttributeError` in templates

**Real-Time Events (Socket.IO):**
- **price_change**: `{product_id, product_name, new_price, store_ids, timestamp}`
- **low_stock**: `{item_id, product_id, store_id, shelf_id, shelfCount, stockCount, totalStoreStock, product_name, store_name, shelf_name, ...}`

---

### **KEY FILES ARCHITECTURE**

**Frontend Modules:**
- `main.js` - Socket.IO listeners, i18n, dark/light mode, buyInventoryItem()
- `store_3d.js` - Three.js scene, grid layout, texture pipeline, overlays
- `maps.js` - Leaflet.js integration
- `style.css` - CSS variables, animations, themes, .btn-buy

**Backend Routing:**
- Entity filters by type on all queries (prevents wrong entity attributes)
- Webhook endpoints: `/notify/price-change`, `/notify/low-stock`

toolSpecificData.prompt: Inspect templates/store_detail.html, templates/stores.html, templates/products.html. Identify current rendering for: capacity unit text, country code/flag duplication in stores table, and location column formatting in products table. Note exact snippets/line ranges and any related CSS/JS dependencies in templates/base.html or static/style.css if relevant. Thoroughness: medium. Read-only.

Inspect static/store_3d.js and any related CSS/HTML used by the Three.js overlay (likely template and style files). Identify: product mesh sizing logic, shelf distribution along Z axis and centering calculations, hover overlay product list rendering, current scroll behavior, and wheel event handling interaction with OrbitControls. Provide concrete findings with file paths and line hints. Thoroughness: thorough. Read-only.
toolSpecificData.result:
Now let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings with concrete file paths and line references: ## Three.js Store 3D Visualization - Thorough Analysis ### **1. PRODUCT MESH SIZING LOGIC** **Location:** [static/store_3d.js](static/store_3d.js#L290-L305) - **Product box geometry:** `BoxGeometry(0.34, 0.28, 0.28)` hardcoded at [line 291](static/store_3d.js#L291) - **Uniform scaling:** Applied at [line 320](static/store_3d.js#L320) as `visual.scale.set(0.86, 0.86, 0.86)` (86% of base size) - **Total scaled dimensions:** ~0.29 × 0.24 × 0.24 units - **Material caching:** [line 302-315](static/store_3d.js#L302-L315) uses `PRODUCT_MATERIAL_CACHE` (Map) keyed by `productId|imageUrl|color` - **Texture loading:** Async loading via `THREE.TextureLoader` [line 322-345](static/store_3d.js#L322-L345) with fallback to solid `colorHex` on failure --- ### **2. SHELF DISTRIBUTION ALONG Z-AXIS & CENTERING** **Location:** [static/store_3d.js](static/store_3d.js#L219-244) - **Grid slots layout constants:** - `SHELF_GRID_COLUMNS = 4` (X-axis) - `SHELF_GRID_LEVELS = 4` (Y-axis, vertical) - `SHELF_GRID_DEPTH = 2` (Z-axis) — **Critical: Only 2 depth positions** - Max capacity: `4 × 4 × 2 = 32` units per shelf - **Z-axis positioning:** - `zSlots = [0.6, -0.1]` at [line 224](static/store_3d.js#L224) — front and back positions - Depth calculation: `depth = index % SHELF_GRID_DEPTH` determines slot (0 or 1) - **X-axis positioning (horizontal centering):** - `xSlots = [-1.18, -0.4, 0.4, 1.18]` at [line 223](static/store_3d.js#L223) - Centered around shelf's local origin (0,0,0) - Column calculation: `column = Math.floor(index / SHELF_GRID_DEPTH) % SHELF_GRID_COLUMNS` - **Y-axis positioning (vertical levels):** - `shelfSurfaceY = [-1.15, -0.32, 0.52, 1.28]` at [line 222](static/store_3d.js#L222) - Product offset above each board: `productYOffset = 0.22` [line 225](static/store_3d.js#L225) - **Shelf grid centering logic:** [static/store_3d.js - buildShelfLayout](static/store_3d.js#L348-370) - Calculates grid dimensions: `columns = Math.ceil(Math.sqrt(totalShelves))` - Centers entire grid: `offsetX = ((columns - 1) * spacingX) / 2` [line 361](static/store_3d.js#L361) - Spacing between shelves: `spacingX = 6, spacingZ = 6` [lines 357-358](static/store_3d.js#L357-L358) --- ### **3. HOVER OVERLAY PRODUCT LIST RENDERING** **Location:** [static/store_3d.js - createOverlayCard()](static/store_3d.js#L430-485) - **HTML structure creation:** [lines 431-465](static/store_3d.js#L431-L465) - Title: `

` [line 436](static/store_3d.js#L436) - List if products exist: `
    ` [line 442](static/store_3d.js#L442) - Each item displays: Product name, shelfCount, stockCount [lines 452-458](static/store_3d.js#L452-L458) - Empty state text: `getTourText('empty')` [line 439](static/store_3d.js#L439) - **Overlay card hover triggering:** - onmouseenter → calls `setHoveredOverlayShelf(shelfId)` and `setHoveredShelf()` [lines 467-469](static/store_3d.js#L467-L469) - onmouseleave → clears hover [line 472-474](static/store_3d.js#L472-L474) - onclick → focuses shelf mesh with `focusShelf()` [lines 476-481](static/store_3d.js#L476-L481) - **Dynamic text refresh on language change:** [static/store_3d.js - refreshOverlayText()](static/store_3d.js#L800-821) - Rebuilds all overlay cards when `app:language-changed` event fires [line 622](static/store_3d.js#L622) --- ### **4. SCROLL/WHEEL BEHAVIOR** **No explicit wheel event handler in store_3d.js** — Delegated entirely to **OrbitControls** **OrbitControls configuration:** [static/store_3d.js - createOrbitControls()](static/store_3d.js#L126-155) - **Damping (smooth deceleration after interaction):** - `enableDamping = true` [line 132](static/store_3d.js#L132) - `dampingFactor = 0.1` [line 133](static/store_3d.js#L133) — Controls how quickly momentum decays - **Zoom speed:** `zoomSpeed = 0.92` [line 139](static/store_3d.js#L139) - Applied to wheel delta (scroll wheel triggers zoom) - Zoom constraints: `minDistance` / `maxDistance` clamped at [line 512-513](static/store_3d.js#L512-L513) - **Pan settings:** - `enablePan = true` [line 134](static/store_3d.js#L134) - `panSpeed = 0.85` [line 140](static/store_3d.js#L140) - `screenSpacePanning = false` [line 135](static/store_3d.js#L135) — Panning moves relative to camera view plane, not screen - **Touch action CSS:** `renderer.domElement.style.touchAction = 'none'` [line 61](static/store_3d.js#L61) - Allows OrbitControls to handle pointer events without browser defaults --- ### **5. WHEEL EVENT & ORBITCONTROLS INTERACTION** **Mouse button mapping:** [static/store_3d.js](static/store_3d.js#L142-145) ``` LEFT → ROTATE MIDDLE → DOLLY (zoom via scroll wheel) RIGHT → PAN ``` **Touch gesture mapping:** [lines 148-150](static/store_3d.js#L148-L150) ``` ONE FINGER → ROTATE TWO FINGERS → DOLLY_PAN (simultaneous zoom + pan) ``` **Bounds-constrained pan:** [static/store_3d.js - clampControlsToBounds()](static/store_3d.js#L871-888) - Pan limits calculated at init: `panLimits = {minX, maxX, minZ, maxZ}` [lines 494-499](static/store_3d.js#L494-L499) - Every frame, camera target clamped to limits [line 831](static/store_3d.js#L831) - Prevents camera escaping shelf grid bounds **Viewport-constrained animation:** [static/store_3d.js - focusShelf()](static/store_3d.js#L758-796) - Focus animation: 700ms easing (cubic) [line 777](static/store_3d.js#L777) - Target position clamped via `clampTargetToLimits()` [line 783](static/store_3d.js#L783) --- ### **6. CSS STYLING FOR OVERLAY & VISUALIZATION** **Visualization container:** [static/style.css](static/style.css#L1301-1328) - **Container styling:** [lines 1302-1327](static/style.css#L1302-L1327) - Min height: 600px - Background: gradient mix with surface color - Border radius: `var(--radius-lg)` (12px) - Canvas: `touch-action: none` (implicit from renderer setup) **Overlay card positioning:** [static/style.css](static/style.css#L1337-1391) - **Base card styling:** [lines 1337-1360](static/style.css#L1337-L1360) - Position: `absolute` - Transform: `translate(-50%, -115%)` — Centers horizontally, offsets above shelf - Backdrop-filter blur: `8px` - Opacity: `0.94` (slightly transparent) - Transition: `0.18s ease` on opacity, transform, border-color, box-shadow - **Hover state (.is-hovered):** [lines 1355-1360](static/style.css#L1355-L1360) - Border color: `var(--primary)` (blue highlight) - Opacity increases to `1` - Transform shifts to `-120%` (moves up slightly) - Box shadow intensifies - **List item layout:** [lines 1378-1391](static/style.css#L1378-L1391) - Grid: 3 columns (name | shelfCount | stockCount) - Font size: `0.78rem`, gap: `0.55rem` - Product name has ellipsis overflow handling --- ### **7. KEY STATE MANAGEMENT** **Global state object `store3DState`:** [static/store_3d.js](static/store_3d.js#L100-119) ```javascript { scene, camera, renderer, controls, // Three.js primitives container, overlayContainer, // DOM elements shelfMeshes: [], // Array of Three.js Mesh objects overlayByShelfId: Map, // Overlay cards keyed by shelf hoveredShelf: null, // Currently hovered mesh hoveredOverlayShelfId: null, // Currently hovered overlay card raycaster, mouse: Vector2, // Raycasting for picking focusAnimation: {startedAt, duration...}, // Active animation state panLimits: {minX, maxX, minZ, maxZ} // Camera pan constraints } ``` --- ### **8. INTERSECTION & PICKING LOGIC** **Raycasting hover detection:** [static/store_3d.js - updateHoveredShelf()](static/store_3d.js#L665-680) - Mouse position normalized to NDC: [lines 648-660](static/store_3d.js#L648-L660) - Raycaster intersects shelf meshes: `raycaster.intersectObjects(shelfMeshes, false)` [line 677](static/store_3d.js#L677) - First intersection selected as hovered shelf **Click-to-focus detection:** [static/store_3d.js - onPointerUp()](static/store_3d.js#L606-621) - Detects click vs drag: displacement ≤8px AND elapsed time ≤350ms [lines 613-614](static/store_3d.js#L613-L614) - Triggers `focusShelf(mesh)` on valid click [line 619](static/store_3d.js#L619) --- ### **9. OVERLAY POSITIONING ALGORITHM** **Per-frame update:** [static/store_3d.js - updateOverlayPositions()](static/store_3d.js#L895-942) ``` For each shelf mesh: 1. Get world position + y offset (2.1 units above mesh) 2. Project to camera's NDC space 3. Frustum culling: hide if outside [-1.2, 1.2] box 4. Convert NDC to screen pixels: x = (projected.x * 0.5 + 0.5) * width y = (projected.y * -0.5 + 0.5) * height 5. Calculate depth priority (closer = higher z-index) 6. Apply boost if hovered (z-index = 4000 vs ~1000) 7. Set CSS left/top/z-index ``` --- ### **10. TEMPLATE STRUCTURE** **HTML containers:** [templates/store_detail.html](templates/store_detail.html#L60-63) ```html
    ``` **Script includes:** [lines 306-307](templates/store_detail.html#L306-L307) - Three.js: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.128.0/examples/js/controls/OrbitControls.js` - Custom: `{{ url_for('static', filename='store_3d.js') }}` --- All findings verified and ready for implementation reference.

response.value: Ya tengo los resultados de documentación y plantillas; ahora voy a leer el informe completo del subagente de Three.js para extraer puntos exactos de layout, overlay y scroll, y con eso cerrar el plan detallado.
Con el análisis técnico ya cerrado, voy a revisar la memoria del repositorio para incorporar decisiones previas (especialmente del 3D y reglas de capacidad) y evitar inconsistencias en el plan.

response.value: Ya consolidé contexto funcional, convenciones y el análisis técnico de plantillas + Three.js, incluyendo memoria histórica del 3D y reglas de capacidad. Ahora redacto el plan completo de Issue #25 con fases, dependencias, archivos exactos y verificación, y lo guardaré en memoria de sesión.

## Plan: Issue #25 Correcciones visuales menores

Aplicaré las 5 correcciones como cambios frontend acotados, separando plantillas y Three.js para minimizar riesgo de regresión. El enfoque mantiene intacta la lógica de negocio y las reglas de capacidad; solo ajusta presentación, distribución visual y comportamiento del overlay.

**Steps**
1. Fase 1, baseline técnico  
1. Confirmar los puntos exactos de render en las 3 plantillas afectadas (unidad, país, ubicaciones).  
2. Confirmar en Three.js dónde tocar tamaño de productos, distribución en Z y overlay scroll/wheel para no romper hover, focus ni OrbitControls.

2. Fase 2, correcciones de plantillas (independiente de 3D)  
1. Corrección 1: cambiar m² por m³ en todos los lugares donde aparezca capacidad en la vista de detalle de almacén.  
2. Corrección 2: en la tabla de almacenes, mostrar una sola vez país en formato bandera + código, sin duplicar código en la fila.  
3. Corrección 3: en la tabla de productos, dejar en la columna de ubicaciones solo el número (sin # ni texto adicional).  
4. Verificar que los cambios no rompen estilos ni i18n general.

3. Fase 3, escena 3D (tamaño y simetría)  
1. Incrementar tamaño efectivo de los objetos de producto en estanterías para mejor visibilidad.  
2. Ajustar slots de profundidad en eje Z para que queden centrados y simétricos respecto al centro local de la estantería.  
3. Validar que se conserva el esquema 4x4x2 (32 unidades) y el mapeo de posiciones.

4. Fase 4, overlay 3D (máximo 4 + scroll + aislamiento wheel)  
1. Limitar visualmente a 4 productos con CSS puro mediante max-height y overflow-y auto.  
2. Mantener scroll interno sin interferir con OrbitControls: detener propagación del wheel en el overlay para evitar zoom de escena al desplazar la lista.  
3. Verificar que siguen funcionando hover, click y focus sobre estanterías.

5. Fase 5, verificación de regresión  
1. Comprobar cada vista afectada (detalle almacén, almacenes, productos).  
2. Comprobar interacciones 3D (hover, click-focus, zoom/pan fuera del overlay, scroll dentro del overlay).  
3. Ejecutar checks automáticos del proyecto si están disponibles.

**Relevant files**
-

— unidad de capacidad y estructura del stage 3D.
-

— render de país en celda (bandera + código sin duplicidad).
-

— formato de valor en columna ubicaciones.
-

— tamaño de objetos, distribución Z, eventos del overlay para wheel.
-

— límite visual de 4 filas y scrollbar del overlay.
-

— referencia del render actual de banderas por country code.

**Verification**
1. En detalle de almacén, la capacidad se ve siempre en m³.  
2. En listado de almacenes, cada fila muestra país una sola vez (bandera + código).  
3. En listado de productos, la columna de ubicaciones muestra solo números.  
4. En 3D, productos más visibles sin clipping severo.  
5. Distribución en Z simétrica al centro de cada estantería.  
6. Overlay muestra 4 productos visibles y permite scroll cuando hay más.  
7. Wheel sobre overlay desplaza la lista sin zoom de escena; wheel fuera del overlay mantiene zoom OrbitControls.  
8. Hover y click-focus siguen correctos.

**Decisions**
- Incluido: solo frontend visual/interacción de Issue #25.  
- Excluido: backend Flask, modelo de datos Orion, validaciones de capacidad y lógica de compra/notificaciones.  
- Estrategia overlay: CSS para límite/scroll + ajuste mínimo de evento wheel, sin paginación ni JS extra de negocio.

Plan guardado en memoria de sesión en /memories/session/plan.md.

modeInstructions.name: Plan
modeInstructions.content:
You are a PLANNING AGENT, pairing with the user to create a detailed, actionable plan.

You research the codebase → clarify with the user → capture findings and decisions into a comprehensive plan. This iterative approach catches edge cases and non-obvious requirements BEFORE implementation begins.

Your SOLE responsibility is planning. NEVER start implementation.

**Current plan**: `/memories/session/plan.md` - update using #tool:vscode/memory .

<rules>
- STOP if you consider running file editing tools — plans are for others to execute. The only write tool you have is #tool:vscode/memory for persisting plans.
- Use #tool:vscode/askQuestions freely to clarify requirements — don't make large assumptions
- Present a well-researched plan with loose ends tied BEFORE implementation
</rules>

<workflow>
Cycle through these phases based on user input. This is iterative, not linear. If the user task is highly ambiguous, do only *Discovery* to outline a draft plan, then move on to alignment before fleshing out the full plan.

## 1. Discovery

Run the *Explore* subagent to gather context, analogous existing features to use as implementation templates, and potential blockers or ambiguities. When the task spans multiple independent areas (e.g., frontend + backend, different features, separate repos), launch **2-3 *Explore* subagents in parallel** — one per area — to speed up discovery.

Update the plan with your findings.

## 2. Alignment

If research reveals major ambiguities or if you need to validate assumptions:
- Use #tool:vscode/askQuestions to clarify intent with the user.
- Surface discovered technical constraints or alternative approaches
- If answers significantly change the scope, loop back to **Discovery**

## 3. Design

Once context is clear, draft a comprehensive implementation plan.

The plan should reflect:
- Structured concise enough to be scannable and detailed enough for effective execution
- Step-by-step implementation with explicit dependencies — mark which steps can run in parallel vs. which block on prior steps
- For plans with many steps, group into named phases that are each independently verifiable
- Verification steps for validating the implementation, both automated and manual
- Critical architecture to reuse or use as reference — reference specific functions, types, or patterns, not just file names
- Critical files to be modified (with full paths)
- Explicit scope boundaries — what's included and what's deliberately excluded
- Reference decisions from the discussion
- Leave no ambiguity

Save the comprehensive plan document to `/memories/session/plan.md` via #tool:vscode/memory, then show the scannable plan to the user for review. You MUST show plan to the user, as the plan file is for persistence only, not a substitute for showing it to the user.

## 4. Refinement

On user input after showing the plan:
- Changes requested → revise and present updated plan. Update `/memories/session/plan.md` to keep the documented plan in sync
- Questions asked → clarify, or use #tool:vscode/askQuestions for follow-ups
- Alternatives wanted → loop back to **Discovery** with new subagent
- Approval given → acknowledge, the user can now use handoff buttons

Keep iterating until explicit approval or handoff.
</workflow>

<plan_style_guide>
```markdown
## Plan: {Title (2-10 words)}

{TL;DR - what, why, and how (your recommended approach).}

**Steps**
1. {Implementation step-by-step — note dependency ("*depends on N*") or parallelism ("*parallel with step N*") when applicable}
2. {For plans with 5+ steps, group steps into named phases with enough detail to be independently actionable}

**Relevant files**
- `{full/path/to/file}` — {what to modify or reuse, referencing specific functions/patterns}

**Verification**
1. {Verification steps for validating the implementation (**Specific** tasks, tests, commands, MCP tools, etc; not generic statements)}

**Decisions** (if applicable)
- {Decision, assumptions, and includes/excluded scope}

**Further Considerations** (if applicable, 1-3 items)
1. {Clarifying question with recommendation. Option A / Option B / Option C}
2. {…}
```

Rules:
- NO code blocks — describe changes, link to files and specific symbols/functions
- NO blocking questions at the end — ask during workflow via #tool:vscode/askQuestions
- The plan MUST be presented to the user, don't just mention the plan file.
</plan_style_guide>

You are an agent—keep going until the user's query is completely resolved before ending your turn. ONLY stop if solved or genuinely blocked.
Take action when possible; the user expects you to do useful work without unnecessary questions.
After any parallel, read-only context gathering, give a concise progress update and what's next.
Avoid repetition across turns: don't restate unchanged plans or sections (like the todo list) verbatim; provide delta updates or only the parts that changed.
Tool batches: You MUST preface each batch with a one-sentence why/what/outcome preamble.
Progress cadence: After 3 to 5 tool calls, or when you create/edit > ~3 files in a burst, report progress.
Requirements coverage: Read the user's ask in full and think carefully. Do not omit a requirement. If something cannot be done with available tools, note why briefly and propose a viable alternative.

</reminderInstructions>

Lee AGENTS.md, PRD.md, architecture.md y data_model.md
para ponerte en contexto del proyecto.

Modo Plan. Issue #25: Correcciones visuales menores.

CORRECCIÓN 1 - Unidad de capacidad incorrecta:
En templates/store_detail.html la capacidad del Store
se muestra con unidades m² cuando debe ser m³.
Cambiar m² por m³ en todos los lugares donde aparezca.

CORRECCIÓN 2 - País duplicado en vista Almacenes:
En templates/stores.html el código de país aparece
duplicado en cada fila: se muestra la bandera y el
código de texto por separado, resultando en dos
valores (ej. "ES" y "ES"). Mostrar solo una vez:
bandera + código en una sola celda sin repetición.

CORRECCIÓN 3 - Símbolo # en valores de tabla Productos:
En templates/products.html las celdas de la columna
de ubicaciones muestran "8 # Ubicaciones" en lugar
de solo el número "8". Eliminar el símbolo # y el
texto "Ubicaciones" del valor de cada celda, dejando
solo el número.

CORRECCIÓN 4 - Representación 3D: tamaño de productos
y distribución:
En static/store_3d.js los objetos 3D que representan
los productos dentro de las estanterías son demasiado
pequeños. Aumentar su tamaño para que sean más visibles
y aprovechen mejor el espacio disponible en cada
estantería. Además, la distribución de las estanterías
en el eje Z no es simétrica: hay más espacio en un lado
que en otro. Corregir para que la distribución sea
centrada y simétrica respecto al centro de la escena.

CORRECCIÓN 5 - Overlay 3D: limitar a 4 productos con
scroll:
En el overlay que aparece al hacer hover sobre una
estantería en la escena Three.js, mostrar un máximo
de 4 productos visibles a la vez. Si hay más de 4
productos en esa estantería, mostrar una barra
deslizante lateral (scrollbar CSS) para ver el resto.
Implementar con CSS puro usando overflow-y: auto y
max-height equivalente a 4 productos, sin JS adicional
y sin interferir con los controles OrbitControls de
la escena 3D. Asegurarse de que el scroll del overlay
no propague el evento al zoom de la escena usando
stopPropagation en el evento wheel del overlay.

Solo elabora el plan. No implementes nada todavía.
</userRequest>

{"command":"create","path":"/memories/session/plan.md","file_text":"## Plan: Issue 25 Visual and 3D Corrections

Aplicar cinco correcciones acotadas de UI y escena 3D sin cambios de lógica de negocio: actualizar unidades de capacidad en detalle de almacén, eliminar duplicidades visuales en tabla de almacenes y formato textual en tabla de productos, y ajustar visibilidad/distribución/overlay en Three.js manteniendo interacciones actuales (hover, click-focus, OrbitControls, i18n/theme).

**Steps**
1. Phase 1: Baseline and impact map
1. Confirmar en plantillas actuales los puntos exactos de render para las tres correcciones HTML: unidad de capacidad en detalle, celda país en listado de almacenes y celda de ubicaciones en listado de productos. Este paso fija el baseline visual y reduce regresiones por cambios fuera de alcance.
1. Revisar en escena 3D los puntos de extensión seguros para tamaño de productos, distribución en eje Z y overlay scroll (creación de geometría/escalado, layout de estanterías, estilos de tarjeta y listeners de overlay). *Bloquea fases 3 y 4*.

2. Phase 2: Template visual corrections (independent from 3D)
1. Corrección 1: reemplazar m² por m³ en todos los lugares de la vista de detalle de almacén donde se muestra capacidad. Validar que no queden sufijos m² en la misma plantilla.
1. Corrección 2: en la celda país de la tabla de almacenes dejar una sola representación visual: bandera + código textual una única vez, evitando repetición del código en la misma fila.
1. Corrección 3: en tabla de productos, columna ubicaciones, dejar únicamente el número entero por celda (sin símbolo # ni etiqueta textual asociada al valor).
1. Revisar impacto i18n mínimo en textos de celdas para no romper traducciones globales ni clases CSS existentes. *Parallel con Phase 3 si no hay conflicto de archivos*.

3. Phase 3: 3D product size and symmetric shelf distribution
1. Aumentar tamaño efectivo de objetos de producto en estanterías ajustando dimensiones base y/o escala visual de mallas para mejorar legibilidad sin interpenetraciones evidentes con baldas.
1. Reequilibrar distribución de productos sobre eje Z para que los slots de profundidad queden centrados y simétricos respecto al centro local de cada estantería (misma separación positiva/negativa alrededor de 0).
1. Verificar que el ajuste preserve invariantes de capacidad física (4x4x2 = 32 unidades) y el mapeo index->slot (columna/nivel/profundidad).

4. Phase 4: 3D overlay max 4 rows with scroll and wheel isolation
1. Limitar visualización simultánea del listado del overlay a 4 productos mediante CSS puro en la lista/tarjeta del overlay usando max-height + overflow-y: auto.
1. Mantener interacción de puntero en overlay sin interferir con escena 3D: agregar manejo explícito de wheel en elemento overlay para stopPropagation (y preventDefault cuando proceda) evitando zoom no deseado en OrbitControls al desplazar la lista.
1. Validar que el scroll se mantiene dentro de la tarjeta (sin desbordes fuera del stage) y que continúa funcionando hover/click-focus de estanterías.

5. Phase 5: Regression verification and docs scope
1. Ejecutar pruebas manuales dirigidas por vista (detalle almacén, listado almacenes, listado productos) y por interacción 3D (hover, click-focus, orbit zoom/pan, scroll overlay con >4 productos).
1. Ejecutar checks automáticos disponibles del proyecto (si existen) para confirmar ausencia de errores de plantilla/JS.
1. Confirmar no-scope: no alterar modelo de datos, validaciones de capacidad backend, ni lógica de compra/notificaciones.

**Relevant files**
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/templates/store_detail.html — sufijo de unidad de capacidad y contenedor del stage 3D.
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/templates/stores.html — celda país con bandera/código y estilos locales de la tabla.
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/templates/products.html — formato de valor en columna de ubicaciones.
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/static/store_3d.js — tamaño de mallas de productos, slots Z, creación de overlay card y manejo de wheel para aislamiento con OrbitControls.
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/static/style.css — estilos de overlay card/list para límite de 4 filas visibles y scrollbar.
- /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app/static/main.js — referencia de render de banderas por country code, para asegurar compatibilidad con la celda de almacenes.

**Verification**
1. Store detail: abrir vista de un almacén y confirmar que la capacidad siempre se muestra en m³ en todos los bloques de la plantilla.
1. Stores list: comprobar que cada fila muestra exactamente una combinación bandera + código (sin duplicación visual del código).
1. Products list: comprobar que la columna ubicaciones muestra solo números enteros por celda.
1. 3D sizing: validar visualmente que los productos son más grandes y legibles, sin clipping severo con geometría de estantería.
1. 3D Z symmetry: revisar estanterías desde vista frontal/lateral para confirmar distribución centrada con separación equilibrada en ambos lados del eje Z.
1. Overlay cap + scroll: usar una estantería con más de 4 productos, verificar que solo 4 filas quedan visibles y aparece scroll vertical funcional.
1. Wheel isolation: al hacer wheel sobre overlay, se desplaza el listado y no se activa zoom de OrbitControls; al hacer wheel fuera del overlay, el zoom de escena sigue operativo.
1. Interacción general: hover de estantería, click en estantería y click en overlay siguen enfocando correctamente sin degradación.

**Decisions**
- Incluido: únicamente correcciones visuales y de interacción frontend solicitadas en Issue #25.
- Excluido: cambios en backend Flask, consultas Orion, reglas de validación de capacidad o estructura de entidades.
- Enfoque técnico recomendado para overlay: resolver límite de elementos con CSS y solo añadir aislamiento de wheel mínimo en JS existente del overlay, sin lógica adicional de paginación ni virtualización.

**Further Considerations**
1. Tolerancia visual de tamaño 3D: usar incremento moderado y validar en desktop + viewport pequeño para evitar saturación visual.
2. Si existen estilos responsivos específicos de tabla, revalidar legibilidad del país combinado (bandera + código) en anchos reducidos.
3. Mantener consistencia con i18n actual: quitar texto en celdas numéricas sin romper labels de cabecera ni traducciones globales."}

- [FIWARE-app/static/main.js](FIWARE-app/static/main.js) — referencia del render actual de banderas por country code.

**Verification**
1. En detalle de almacén, la capacidad se ve siempre en m³.  
2. En listado de almacenes, cada fila muestra país una sola vez (bandera + código).  
3. En listado de productos, la columna de ubicaciones muestra solo números.  
4. En 3D, productos más visibles sin clipping severo.  
5. Distribución en Z simétrica al centro de cada estantería.  
6. Overlay muestra 4 productos visibles y permite scroll cuando hay más.  
7. Wheel sobre overlay desplaza la lista sin zoom de escena; wheel fuera del overlay mantiene zoom OrbitControls.  
8. Hover y click-focus siguen correctos.

**Decisions**
- Incluido: solo frontend visual/interacción de Issue #25.  
- Excluido: backend Flask, modelo de datos Orion, validaciones de capacidad y lógica de compra/notificaciones.  
- Estrategia overlay: CSS para límite/scroll + ajuste mínimo de evento wheel, sin paginación ni JS extra de negocio.

Plan guardado en memoria de sesión en /memories/session/plan.md.

## Summary

### Files Examined
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)
- [PRD.md](PRD.md)
- [architecture.md](architecture.md)
- [data_model.md](data_model.md)

---

### **CONSTRAINTS**

**Model & Data Coherence:**
- `shelfCount ≤ stockCount` (always)
- `stockCount` = sum of all `shelfCount` for (Product, Store)
- `numberOfItems` = sum of `shelfCount` values for that Shelf (coherence validation)
- **Each Shelf MUST contain MINIMUM 4 distinct Products**
- Orion 4.1.0: All numeric types use `"type": "Number"` (not `"Integer"`)
- Orion 4.1.0: String values **ASCII only** (no accents: "Maria" not "María", "Almacen" not "Almacén")
- Orion 4.1.0: Image URLs without query params (`no ?w=400`)

**Shelf Capacity (Physical):**
- Max capacity per Shelf: **32 units** (grid layout 4×4×2)
- `maxCapacity` must be 0 < maxCapacity ≤ 32
- Cannot reduce `maxCapacity` below current occupation
- Occupation = sum of all `shelfCount` on that Shelf
- Validation: `occupation_shelf + new_units ≤ maxCapacity_shelf`

**Store & Location:**
- `countryCode`: exactly 2 characters [A-Za-z] (ISO 2-char format: ES, FR, IT)
- `location`: GeoJSON Point [lon, lat] valid WGS84
- `temperature`, `relativeHumidity`, `tweets`: **READ-ONLY** (from external providers, no client modification)

**InventoryItem:**
- `shelfCount ≤ 0`: Button "Comprar" disabled
- Stock change (buy): Direct PATCH from client to Orion (no Flask intermediation)
- **Low stock trigger**: `shelfCount < 3` → fires `low_stock` notification

**Webhooks (Critical):**
- Use `http://host.docker.internal:5000/notify/...` (**NOT** `localhost`)

**NGSIv2 Format (Rigid):**
- All attributes: `{"type": "Text|Number|etc", "value": ...}`
- Pattern matching: Use `"idPattern": ".*"` (not `"isPattern": true`)

---

### **CONVENTIONS**

**Identifiers (Orion URN):**
- Format: `urn:ngsi-ld:<EntityType>:<UniqueIdentifier>`
- Case: kebab-case for identifiers (e.g., `urn:ngsi-ld:Store:madrid-centro`)

**Routes (Flask):**
- Pattern: `/<Entity>/<action>` or `/<Entity>/<id>/<action>`
- HTTP methods: standard REST (GET, POST, PATCH)

**Variables:**
- Python: 4-space indent, 100-char max line, snake_case
- JavaScript: camelCase variables, UPPER_CASE constants, 2-space indent, `const` preferred, arrow functions
- CSS: kebab-case with prefixes, 2-space indent, CSS custom properties (`--variable-name`)
- HTML/Jinja2: 2-space indent, semantic tags, `data-i18n` for visible text, `data-*` for JS hooks

**Templates:**
- Pattern: `<Entity>-<action>.html` or `<Entity>.html`

**Documents:**
- Update after each feature/fix merge: `PRD.md`, `architecture.md`, `data_model.md`

---

### **UI / 3D / STORE CAPACITY / COUNTRY / PRODUCTS TABLE / 3D OVERLAY**

**Store Detail - View Layout:**
- Photo, sensors (temperature/humidity with color-coded indicators)
- **Leaflet.js map** with store markers and environmental tooltips
- **Three.js 3D traversal** with floor plan + interactive shelves
- Inventory table grouped by shelf with progress bars
- Tweets panel
- Real-time notifications panel
- Environmental data from external providers

**3D Traversal (Issue #22):**
- **Grid Layout**: 4×4×2 (columns × levels × depth) = max 32 units per shelf
- **Representation**: Each `shelfCount` unit = independent 3D cube
- **Texture Pipeline**:
  - Initial: `MeshStandardMaterial` with `Product.color` (hex #RRGGBB)
  - On texture load: assign `material.map`, force `material.color = #FFFFFF` (anti-tinting), enable `needsUpdate`
  - On texture error: keep color fallback (no degradation)
- **Interaction**:
  - OrbitControls (rotate, zoom/dolly, pan)
  - Hover: shelf highlight
  - Click/tap: camera centers on shelf with smooth animation
- **Overlay (2D per shelf)**:
  - Product name + shelfCount + stockCount per product
  - Dynamic centering from real shelf positions
  - Pan limits in X/Z to prevent scene loss
  - Depth-based overlay prioritization
  - **Strict clipping**: overlays contained within 3D container (no navbar invasion)
  - Resolved via `products_dict` (no extra requests)

**Products Table:**
- Columns: image, name, price, size, color, stock (total), quantity_on_shelf
- Real-time price updates via Socket.IO (`price_change` event)
- "Comprar" button per InventoryItem
  - Disabled if `shelfCount ≤ 0`
  - Direct PATCH to Orion (no reload)

**Store List (Country Display):**
- Columns/fields: photo, name, **country** (from `countryCode`), temperature (color-coded), humidity (color-coded)
- Temperature/humidity indicators use color thresholds for visual feedback

**Data Query Pattern (Critical - Issue #9):**
- Always include **critical attributes** in `include_attrs`, not just filters:
  - Example: `"name,temperature,relativeHumidity,tweets"` (include `name` even if filtering other attrs)
  - Omitting `name` causes `AttributeError` in templates

**Real-Time Events (Socket.IO):**
- **price_change**: `{product_id, product_name, new_price, store_ids, timestamp}`
- **low_stock**: `{item_id, product_id, store_id, shelf_id, shelfCount, stockCount, totalStoreStock, product_name, store_name, shelf_name, ...}`

---

### **KEY FILES ARCHITECTURE**

**Frontend Modules:**
- `main.js` - Socket.IO listeners, i18n, dark/light mode, buyInventoryItem()
- `store_3d.js` - Three.js scene, grid layout, texture pipeline, overlays
- `maps.js` - Leaflet.js integration
- `style.css` - CSS variables, animations, themes, .btn-buy

**Backend Routing:**
- Entity filters by type on all queries (prevents wrong entity attributes)
- Webhook endpoints: `/notify/price-change`, `/notify/low-stock`

Here's the content of /memories/repo/issue_14_3d_store_analysis.md with line numbers:
     1	# Issue #14: 3D Store Scene Analysis - Thorough Mapping
     2	
     3	## 1. ENTRY POINTS AND INITIALIZATION
     4	
     5	### Global Entry Point
     6	- **Driver:** [store_detail.html#L308-L317](store_detail.html#L308-L317)
     7	- **Function:** `window.initializeStoreScene(config)`
     8	- **Export:** [store_3d.js#L836](store_3d.js#L836)
     9	
    10	### Initialization Parameters (Data Contract)
    11	```javascript
    12	{
    13	  storeId: "urn:ngsi-ld:Store:...",
    14	  shelves: Array<Shelf>,                    // Array of entities
    15	  inventoryByShelf: {                       // Keyed by shelf.id
    16	    "shelf_id": [InventoryItem, ...]
    17	  },
    18	  productsById: {                           // Keyed by product.id for O(1) lookup
    19	    "product_id": Product
    20	  }
    21	}
    22	```
    23	
    24	### Config Sources
    25	- `window.inventoryData` populated by Flask template at render in store_detail.html
    26	- Critical data: `shelves`, `inventory_by_shelf`, `products_dict`
    27	
    28	---
    29	
    30	## 2. CORE THREE.JS RENDERING PIPELINE
    31	
    32	### Scene Initialization: `initializeStoreScene()` [Line 23-96]
    33	
    34	#### A. Core Scene Graph Setup
    35	| Element | Constructor | Properties | Purpose |
    36	|---------|-------------|-----------|---------|
    37	| Scene | `new THREE.Scene()` | background color | Root container |
    38	| Camera | `THREE.PerspectiveCamera(58°, aspect, 0.1, 1500)` | FOV=58°, near=0.1, far=1500 | Perspective view |
    39	| Renderer | `THREE.WebGLRenderer({antialias: true})` | shadowMap enabled, pixel ratio clamped ≤2 | GPU rendering |
    40	
    41	#### B. Lighting Setup
    42	1. **Ambient Light** [Line 54-55]
    43	   - Intensity: `0.6`
    44	   - Color: `0xffffff` (white)
    45	   - Purpose: Base illumination
    46	
    47	2. **Directional Light** [Line 57-60]
    48	   - Position: `(12, 22, 14)` – angled from upper-right-front
    49	   - Intensity: `0.9`
    50	   - CastShadow: `true`
    51	   - Purpose: Primary illumination + shadow generation
    52	
    53	#### C. Floor Mesh [Line 62-72]
    54	- Geometry: `PlaneGeometry(140, 140)` – 140x140 world units
    55	- Material: `MeshStandardMaterial`
    56	  - Color: `0xced4da` (light gray)
    57	  - Roughness: `0.9`
    58	  - Metalness: `0.08`
    59	- Transform: Rotated -90° on X-axis (vertical to floor)
    60	- ReceiveShadow: `true`
    61	- Purpose: Ground plane for spatial reference
    62	
    63	### State Object: `store3DState` [Line 73-94]
    64	Central singleton managing all render state:
    65	
    66	```javascript
    67	{
    68	  scene,                              // THREE.Scene
    69	  camera,                             // THREE.Camera
    70	  renderer,                           // THREE.WebGLRenderer
    71	  controls,                           // OrbitControls instance
    72	  container,                          // DOM #three-container
    73	  overlayContainer,                   // DOM #three-overlay
    74	  inventoryByShelf: {},               // shelf_id -> [items]
    75	  productsById: {},                   // product_id -> Product
    76	  shelfMeshes: [],                    // Array of THREE.Mesh (boxes)
    77	  overlayByShelfId: Map,              // shelfId -> {element, shelfName, rows}
    78	  raycaster,                          // THREE.Raycaster for picking
    79	  mouse,                              // THREE.Vector2
    80	  hoveredShelf: null,                 // Current hovered mesh
    81	  hoveredOverlayShelfId: null,        // Current hovered overlay shelf
    82	  animationFrameId: null,             // RAF handle
    83	  handlers: {},                       // Event handler references
    84	  bounds: null,                       // {minX, maxX, minZ, maxZ, center, radius}
    85	  panLimits: null,                    // {minX, maxX, minZ, maxZ}
    86	  pointerDown: null,                  // {x, y, time, pointerId}
    87	  focusAnimation: null                // {startedAt, durationMs, ...}
    88	}
    89	```
    90	
    91	---
    92	
    93	## 3. CAMERA CONTROLS (OrbitControls)
    94	
    95	### Configuration: `createOrbitControls()` [Line 102-127]
    96	
    97	| Setting | Value | Impact |
    98	|---------|-------|--------|
    99	| `enableDamping` | `true` | Inertial motion after mouse release |
   100	| `dampingFactor` | `0.1` | Smooth deceleration curve |
   101	| `enablePan` | `true` | Allow panning with right-mouse/middle-button |
   102	| `screenSpacePanning` | `false` | Pan moves relative to world, not screen |
   103	| `minPolarAngle` | `0.35 rad` (≈20°) | Prevent viewing from below floor |
   104	| `maxPolarAngle` | `π/2 - 0.06` (≈87°) | Prevent bird's-eye view |
   105	| `rotateSpeed` | `0.72` | Moderate rotation sensitivity |
   106	| `zoomSpeed` | `0.92` | Moderate zoom sensitivity |
   107	| `panSpeed` | `0.85` | Moderate pan sensitivity |
   108	| `mouseButtons` | LEFT=ROTATE, MIDDLE=DOLLY, RIGHT=PAN | Standard 3-button configuration |
   109	| `touches` | ONE=ROTATE, TWO=DOLLY_PAN | Mobile support |
   110	
   111	### Distance Bounds (Dynamic)
   112	- **minDistance:** `max(5, radius * 0.65)` – prevents too-close zoom
   113	- **maxDistance:** `max(minDistance + 10, radius * 4.2)` – prevents too-far zoom
   114	
   115	### Focus View: `configureInitialView()` [Line 256-284]
   116	- Computes bounds of all shelves
   117	- Positions camera at `[center.x + 0.85*fitDist, center.y + 0.65*fitDist, center.z + 0.85*fitDist]`
   118	- Sets controls target to scene center
   119	- Clamps pan to `panLimits` (shelves ± 7 units)
   120	
   121	---
   122	
   123	## 4. SHELF MESH RENDERING PIPELINE
   124	
   125	### Mesh Creation: `createShelfMeshes()` [Line 131-196]
   126	
   127	#### A. Layout Calculation: `buildShelfLayout()` [Line 198-224]
   128	```
   129	Given N shelves:
   130	  1. columns = ceil(sqrt(N))
   131	  2. rows = ceil(N / columns)
   132	  3. Grid spacing: 6 units X and Z
   133	  4. Center grid on origin
   134	  5. Return array of {x, z} positions
   135	```
   136	Example: 6 shelves → 3×2 grid
   137	
   138	#### B. Per-Shelf Processing
   139	For each shelf in config.shelves:
   140	1. Get layout position `{x, z}`
   141	2. Get shelf name (or fallback: `"Shelf ${index+1}"`)
   142	3. Retrieve `inventoryByShelf[shelfId]` (array of InventoryItems)
   143	4. Build product rows using `buildShelfRows()`
   144	5. Create THREE.Mesh
   145	6. Create DOM overlay card
   146	7. Register in `overlayByShelfId` Map
   147	
   148	#### C. Three.Mesh Properties for Each Shelf
   149	| Property | Value | Significance |
   150	|----------|-------|--------------|
   151	| Geometry | `BoxGeometry(3.2, 2.4, 1.6)` | Wall-mounted shelf dimensions |
   152	| Material | `MeshStandardMaterial` | Metallic shelf appearance |
   153	| Color | `0x1f73db` (blue) | Primary shelf color |
   154	| Roughness | `0.45` | Moderately reflective |
   155	| Metalness | `0.12` | Slight metallic sheen |
   156	| Emissive | `0x000000` (initially) | No glow initially |
   157	| castShadow | `true` | Projects shadows |
   158	| receiveShadow | `true` | Receives projection shadows |
   159	| userData.shelfId | shelf ID string | Business data attachment |
   160	| Position | `(x, 1.2, z)` | Y=1.2 (eye-level height) |
   161	
   162	#### D. Product Data Assembly: `buildShelfRows()` [Line 226-241]
   163	For each InventoryItem in shelf:
   164	```javascript
   165	{
   166	  name: product.name.value || fallback,
   167	  shelfCount: numeric(item.shelfCount),    // Units on THIS shelf
   168	  stockCount: numeric(item.stockCount)     // Total units in store
   169	}
   170	```
   171	
   172	---

   174	## 5. HOVER HIGHLIGHTING MECHANISM
   175	
   176	### Visual Feedback: `setHoveredShelf()` [Line 450-468]
   177	
   178	#### Before (Previous Shelf)
   179	```javascript
   180	if (prev && prev.material) {
   181	  prev.material.emissive.setHex(0x000000)  // Remove glow
   182	  // Remove overlay CSS class unless overlay is also hovered
   183	}
   184	```
   185	
   186	#### After (New Shelf)
   187	```javascript
   188	if (mesh && mesh.material) {
   189	  mesh.material.emissive.setHex(0x0f4f9f)  // Add glow (dark blue)
   190	  // Add overlay CSS class .is-hovered
   191	}
   192	```
   193	
   194	### CSS Overlay Hover State: `.shelf-overlay-card.is-hovered` [CSS Line 1184-1187]
   195	```css
   196	border-color: var(--primary);           /* Change border to primary blue */
   197	opacity: 1;                              /* Full opacity */
   198	transform: translate(-50%, -120%);       /* Move up additional 5px */
   199	```
   200	
   201	### Hybrid Hover Behavior
   202	- **Mesh hover** (raycasting): Updates 3D material emissive color
   203	- **Overlay card hover** (DOM mouseover): Updates CSS class
   204	- **Bidirectional:** Hovering either affects both (via `setHoveredShelf()` and `setHoveredOverlayShelf()`)
   205	
   206	### Event Handlers
   207	
   208	#### Mesh Hover Detection: `updateHoveredShelf()` [Line 442-446]
   209	1. Raycasts from mouse position through camera
   210	2. Finds first intersected shelf mesh
   211	3. Calls `setHoveredShelf()` with result
   212	
   213	#### Overlay Card Hover: `createOverlayCard()` [Line 257-281]
   214	```javascript
   215	card.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
   216	  setHoveredOverlayShelf(shelfId);
   217	  setHoveredShelf(findShelfMeshById(shelfId));
   218	});
   219	
   220	card.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
   221	  setHoveredOverlayShelf(null);
   222	  // Mesh hover state handled separately
   223	});
   224	```
   225

   227	
   228	## 6. OVERLAY RENDERING AND POSITIONING
   229	
   230	### DOM Structure
   231	```
   232	#three-container (THREE.js canvas + renderer)
   233	#three-overlay (absolute positioned container)
   234	  └─ .shelf-overlay-card * N (one per shelf)
   235	      ├─ h4.shelf-overlay-title
   236	      └─ ul.shelf-overlay-list
   237	          └─ li.shelf-overlay-item * M (one per product)
   238	              ├─ span.label (product name)
   239	              ├─ span (shelf count label)
   240	              └─ span (stock count label)
   241	```
   242	
   243	### Overlay Card Creation: `createOverlayCard()` [Line 243-281]
   244	- DOM structure built once when scene initializes
   245	- One card per unique shelfId
   246	- Content regenerated on language change only (not per-frame)
   247	
   248	### Projection to Screen: `updateOverlayPositions()` [Line 676-726]
   249	**Runs every animation frame** to maintain correct screen-space positioning:
   250	
   251	1. For each shelf mesh:
   252	   - Get world position at `mesh.position + (0, 2.1, 0)` (above shelf center)
   253	   - Project to camera space using `camera.project(Vector3)`
   254	   - Test NDC (normalized device coordinates) visibility:
   255	     - `z ∈ (-1, 1)` – within near/far planes
   256	     - `x ∈ (-1.2, 1.2)` – within horizontal safezone
   257	     - `y ∈ (-1.2, 1.2)` – within vertical safezone
   258	   
   259	2. If out of bounds → `display: none`
   260	   
   261	3. If visible → Calculate screen coordinates:
   262	   ```javascript
   263	   x = (projected.x * 0.5 + 0.5) * containerWidth
   264	   y = (projected.y * -0.5 + 0.5) * containerHeight
   265	   ```
   266	
   267	4. Calculate z-index based on depth:
   268	   ```javascript
   269	   normalizedDepth = clamp((projected.z + 1) * 0.5, 0, 1)
   270	   depthPriority = round((1 - normalizedDepth) * 1000)
   271	   zIndex = hover ? 4000 : 1000 + depthPriority
   272	   ```
   273	   - Farther overlays rendered behind
   274	   - Hovered overlay always on top (z-index 4000)
   275	
   276	5. Apply CSS transforms:
   277	   ```css
   278	   left: ${x}px;
   279	   top: ${y}px;
   280	   z-index: ${zIndex};
   281	   ```
   282	
   283	### CSS Transform Offsets [CSS Line 1167-1172]
   284	```css
   285	transform: translate(-50%, -115%);      /* Center horizontally, position above */
   286	transition: opacity 0.18s ease, ...;    /* Smooth hover transitions */
   287	opacity: 0.94;                          /* Slightly transparent initially */
   288	```
   289	
   290	---
   291	
   292	## 7. FOCUS ANIMATION (Camera Centering)
   293	
   294	### Trigger: `focusShelf()` [Line 470-510]
   295	
   296	#### When Activated
   297	- User clicks/taps on shelf mesh (< 8px movement, < 350ms duration)
   298	- User clicks overlay card
   299	
   300	#### Animation Setup
   301	```javascript
   302	focusAnimation = {
   303	  startedAt: performance.now(),        // Current timestamp
   304	  durationMs: 700,                     // Animation length
   305	  startTarget: controls.target.clone(), // Current orbital center
   306	  endTarget: clampedMeshPosition,      // Target shelf position
   307	  startPosition: camera.position.clone(), // Current camera position
   308	  endPosition: endTarget + offsetDirection * distance
   309	}
   310	```
   311	
   312	#### End Position Calculation
   313	```
   314	direction = normalize(currentCamPos - currentTarget)
   315	direction.y = max(0.4, direction.y)    // Ensure some upward angle
   316	endTarget = mesh.position + (0, 1.2, 0) // Shelf center at eye height
   317	desiredDistance = clamp(bounds.radius * 1.7, 
   318	                        minDist * 1.2, 
   319	                        maxDist * 0.72)
   320	endPosition = endTarget + direction * desiredDistance
   321	endPosition.y = max(endTarget.y + 3.8, endPosition.y)
   322	```
   323	
   324	### Animation Update: `updateFocusAnimation()` [Line 614-630]
   325	Runs in animation loop:
   326	```javascript
   327	progress = clamp(elapsed / 700, 0, 1)
   328	eased = easeInOutCubic(progress)  // Smooth acceleration/deceleration
   329	
   330	controls.target.lerpVectors(startTarget, endTarget, eased)
   331	camera.position.lerpVectors(startPosition, endPosition, eased)
   332	
   333	if (progress >= 1) focusAnimation = null
   334	```
   335	
   336	#### Easing Function: `easeInOutCubic()` [Line 632-636]
   337	- Smooth cubic interpolation
   338	- Fast start, slow end
   339	- Standard ease-in-out cubic: `4t³` for t<0.5, else `1 - (1-2t)³/2`
   340	
   341	---
   342	
   343	## 8. EVENT HANDLING AND INPUT
   344	
   345	### Event Binding: `bind3DEvents()` [Line 368-412]
   346	
   347	| Event | Handler | Attached To | Purpose |
   348	|-------|---------|------------|---------|
   349	| `mousemove` | `onMouseMove()` | container | Update hover state every frame |
   350	| `mouseleave` | `onMouseLeave()` | container | Clear hover on mouse exit |
   351	| `pointerdown` | `onPointerDown()` | container | Record pointer origin for click detection |
   352	| `pointerup` | `onPointerUp()` | container | Detect click vs drag [Line 386-401] |
   353	| `resize` | `onResize()` | window | Update camera aspect ratio |
   354	| `app:language-changed` | `onLanguageChanged()` | document | Regenerate overlay text |
   355	| `app:theme-changed` | `onThemeChanged()` | document | Update scene background color |
   356	
   357	### Click Detection: `onPointerUp()` [Line 386-401]
   358	```javascript
   359	// Only treat as click if:
   360	//   - Pointer moved ≤ 8px
   361	//   - Time elapsed ≤ 350ms
   362	if (moved ≤ 8px && elapsed ≤ 350ms) {
   363	  intersected = getIntersectedShelf()
   364	  if (intersected) focusShelf(intersected)
   365	}
   366	```
   367	
   368	### Overlay Card Click [Line 277-280]
   369	```javascript
   370	card.addEventListener('click', (event) => {
   371	  event.preventDefault()
   372	  mesh = findShelfMeshById(shelfId)
   373	  if (mesh) focusShelf(mesh)
   374	})
   375	```
   376	
   377	### Mouse Coordinate Transform: `setPointerFromEvent()` [Line 414-425]
   378	Converts DOM event coordinates to NDC (normalized device coordinates -1 to +1):
   379	```javascript
   380	mouse.x = ((event.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 2 - 1
   381	mouse.y = -((event.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 2 + 1
   382	```
   383	
   384	---
   385	
   386	## 9. ANIMATION LOOP AND RENDERING
   387	
   388	### Main Loop: `animateStoreScene()` [Line 638-651]
   389	**Runs via `requestAnimationFrame` every ~16.67ms (60 FPS):**
   390	
   391	```
   392	updateFocusAnimation()      // Update camera during focus animation
   393	controls.update()           // Apply damping, inertia
   394	clampControlsToBounds()    // Enforce pan limits
   395	updateOverlayPositions()   // Project shelf meshes to screen coords
   396	renderer.render(scene, camera)  // WebGL render to canvas
   397	requestAnimationFrame(animateStoreScene)  // Queue next frame
   398	```
   399	
   400	### Render Pipeline Order
   401	1. **Update phase:** Focus animation, controls, overlay positions
   402	2. **Render phase:** WebGL renderer draws scene to canvas
   403	3. **Display:** Browser composites canvas + overlay container
   404	
   405	---
   406	
   407	## 10. CLEANUP AND LIFECYCLE
   408	
   409	### Cleanup: `cleanupStoreScene()` [Line 780-836]
   410	Called:
   411	- When scene is re-initialized
   412	- On page unload (implicit)
   413	
   414	**Operations:**
   415	1. Remove all event listeners:
   416	   - mousemove, mouseleave, pointerdown, pointerup (from container)
   417	   - resize (from window)
   418	   - app:language-changed, app:theme-changed (from document)
   419	
   420	2. Cancel animation frame (`cancelAnimationFrame()`)
   421	
   422	3. Dispose Three.js objects:
   423	   - All shelf geometries and materials
   424	   - OrbitControls instance
   425	   - Renderer (dispose WebGL context, remove canvas from DOM)
   426	
   427	4. Clear DOM:
   428	   - Empty overlay container (`innerHTML = ''`)
   429	
   430	5. Null out global state (`store3DState = null`)
   431	
   432	**Regression risk:** If cleanup not called before re-init, previous scene lingers in memory.
   433	
   434	---

   438	### Language Change: `refreshOverlayText()` [Line 552-597]
   439	- Triggered by `app:language-changed` event
   440	- Rebuilds overlay card DOM content only (retains positions)
   441	- Uses `getTourText(key)` to fetch localized strings

   443	### Theme Change: `updateSceneTheme()` [Line 599-606]
   444	- Triggered by `app:theme-changed` event
   445	- Updates `scene.background` color based on `body.dark-theme`
   446	- Dark: `0x1c1f24` (dark gray)
   447	- Light: `0xf2f4f8` (very light gray)

   673	
   674	## 14. REGRESSION RISKS AND CRITICAL INVARIANTS
   675	
   676	### Critical Invariants (Do Not Break)
   677	
   678	#### A. State Object Integrity
   679	- `store3DState` is singleton – only one scene at a time
   680	- Cleanup must fully dispose `store3DState` before re-init
   681	- **Risk if broken:** Memory leaks, orphaned event listeners, canvas not removed
   682	
   683	#### B. Raycasting Accuracy
   684	- Mouse-to-world transformation in `setPointerFromEvent()` [Line 414-425] must remain synchronized with camera+renderer
   685	- Raycaster queries only `shelfMeshes` array, not all scene objects
   686	- **Risk if broken:** Clicking empty space or wrong shelf, hover doesn't work
   687	
   688	#### C. Overlay Projection Algorithm
   689	- Projection matrix must match camera exactly (happens automatically via `camera.project()`)
   690	- Z-index calculation must account for depth-sorting correctly
   691	- **Risk if broken:** Overlays in wrong position, wrong z-order, text unreadable
   692	
   693	#### D. Click Detection Tolerance
   694	- 8px movement threshold and 350ms duration [Line 388-389] are tuned for mobile/touch
   695	- **Risk if changed:** Accidental clicks during pan, false positives
   696	
   697	#### E. Pan Limits Enforcement
   698	- `clampTargetToLimits()` [Line 658-663] must apply before controls update
   699	- Limits must be recomputed on shelf layout changes
   700	- **Risk if broken:** Camera can pan into empty space, loss of scene context
   701	
   702	#### F. Event Handler Cleanup
   703	- All handlers registered in `bind3DEvents()` [Line 370-383] must be deregistered in `cleanupStoreScene()`
   704	- **Risk if broken:** Multiple scene initializations leak event listeners, erratic behavior
   705	
   706	#### G. Three.js Disposal
   707	- Geometries, materials, controls, renderer must call `.dispose()` [Line 808-828]
   708	- Canvas must be removed from DOM
   709	- **Risk if broken:** WebGL context exhaustion, memory leaks, GPU resource starvation
   710	
   711	#### H. Language/Theme Consistency
   712	- Text strings fetched via `getTourText()` [Line 738-745] must be defined in `TOUR_TEXT` object [Line 7-19]
   713	- Scene background must respond to `app:theme-changed` event
   714	- **Risk if broken:** Untranslated text, theme mismatch, console errors
   715	
   716	### Common Regression Vectors
   717	
   718	| Action | Risk Level | Mitigation |
   719	|--------|-----------|-----------|
   720	| Change shelf position calculation (buildShelfLayout) | HIGH | Retest camera fit, pan limits, raycasting |
   721	| Modify raycaster setup or mouse transform | HIGH | Test click accuracy, hover at edges, mobile touch |
   722	| Add/remove scene objects | MEDIUM | Retest raycasting (only shelfMeshes should intersect) |
   723	| Change animation timing (focus duration, damping) | MEDIUM | Test on low-end device, TDD with manual QA |
   724	| Modify overlay positioning formula | HIGH | Test projection at various camera angles, zoom levels |
   725	| Change material colors dynamically | MEDIUM | Test hover highlighting remains visible |
   726	| Resize visualization container | MEDIUM | Test camera projection, overlay clipping |
   727	| Language/theme changes mid-scene | LOW | Ensure text rebuilds correctly, background updates |
   728	
   729	---
   730	
   731	## 15. FILE REFERENCES & LINE MAPPINGS
   732	
   733	### Key Functions
   734	
   735	| Function | File | Lines | Purpose |
   736	|----------|------|-------|---------|
   737	| `initializeStoreScene()` | store_3d.js | 23–96 | Entry point, initializes scene |
   738	| `createOrbitControls()` | store_3d.js | 102–127 | Camera control setup |
   739	| `createShelfMeshes()` | store_3d.js | 131–196 | Build shelf geometry |
   740	| `buildShelfLayout()` | store_3d.js | 198–224 | Calculate grid positions |
   741	| `buildShelfRows()` | store_3d.js | 226–241 | Prepare product data for overlay |
   742	| `createOverlayCard()` | store_3d.js | 243–281 | Create DOM overlay element |
   743	| `configureInitialView()` | store_3d.js | 283–317 | Set initial camera position |
   744	| `computeShelvesBounds()` | store_3d.js | 319–351 | Calculate bounding box |
   745	| `bind3DEvents()` | store_3d.js | 368–412 | Register all event handlers |
   746	| `setPointerFromEvent()` | store_3d.js | 414–425 | Transform mouse coords to NDC |
   747	| `getIntersectedShelf()` | store_3d.js | 427–436 | Raycast to find hovered shelf |
   748	| `updateHoveredShelf()` | store_3d.js | 438–441 | Detect and update hover state |
   749	| `setHoveredOverlayShelf()` | store_3d.js | 442–458 | Update overlay hover CSS class |
   750	| `setHoveredShelf()` | store_3d.js | 460–483 | Update mesh emissive glow |
   751	| `focusShelf()` | store_3d.js | 485–510 | Trigger focus animation |
   752	| `refreshOverlayText()` | store_3d.js | 540–597 | Regenerate overlay text (language) |
   753	| `updateSceneTheme()` | store_3d.js | 599–606 | Update background color (theme) |
   754	| `animateStoreScene()` | store_3d.js | 608–613 | Main animation loop |
   755	| `updateFocusAnimation()` | store_3d.js | 615–630 | Update camera during focus animation |
   756	| `easeInOutCubic()` | store_3d.js | 632–636 | Easing function |
   757	| `clampControlsToBounds()` | store_3d.js | 638–651 | Enforce pan limits |
   758	| `clampTargetToLimits()` | store_3d.js | 653–663 | Clamp orbit target to bounds |
   759	| `updateOverlayPositions()` | store_3d.js | 665–726 | Project shelves to screen space |
   760	| `onStoreSceneResize()` | store_3d.js | 728–745 | Handle window resize |
   761	| `findShelfMeshById()` | store_3d.js | 747–754 | Lookup shelf by ID |
   762	| `getSceneBackgroundColor()` | store_3d.js | 756–759 | Theming helper |
   763	| `getTourText()` | store_3d.js | 761–768 | i18n helper |
   764	| `cleanupStoreScene()` | store_3d.js | 770–836 | Teardown and disposal |
   765	
   766	### CSS Classes
   767	
   768	| Class | File | Lines | Purpose |
   769	|-------|------|-------|---------|
   770	| `.store-3d-stage` | style.css | 1136–1142 | Container for 3D scene |
   771	| `.visualization-container` | style.css | 1143–1152 | Canvas wrapper |
   772	| `.store-3d-overlay` | style.css | 1160–1165 | Overlay positioning wrapper |
   773	| `.shelf-overlay-card` | style.css | 1167–1181 | Base card styling |
   774	| `.shelf-overlay-card.is-hovered` | style.css | 1184–1187 | Hover state |
   775	| `.shelf-overlay-title` | style.css | 1190–1195 | Card heading |
   776	| `.shelf-overlay-list` | style.css | 1197–1203 | Product list container |
   777	| `.shelf-overlay-item` | style.css | 1205–1212 | Product row styling |
   778	| `.shelf-overlay-item .label` | style.css | 1214–1218 | Product name text |
   779	| `.shelf-overlay-empty` | style.css | 1220–1224 | Empty state message |
   780	
   781	### DOM IDs
   782	
   783	| ID | Location | Purpose |
   784	|----|----------|---------|
   785	| `#three-container` | store_detail.html:62 | Canvas render target |
   786	| `#three-overlay` | store_detail.html:63 | Overlay card container |
   787	| `#store-detail-root` | store_detail.html:6 | Page root (unused by 3D scene) |
   788	
   789	---
   790	
   791	## 16. PERFORMANCE AND SCALABILITY NOTES
   792	
   793	### Current Bottlenecks
   794	
   795	1. **Overlay Projection Every Frame [Line 665-726]**
   796	   - O(N) where N = number of shelves
   797	   - Runs 60 times per second
   798	   - DOM manipulation (style updates) may cause reflow
   799	   - Mitigation: Consider caching computed positions, batch DOM updates
   800	
   801	2. **Material Update on Hover [Line 450-468]**
   802	   - Direct material color modification
   803	   - Resets emissive value per state change
   804	   - Safe, but could batch updates if supporting multiple highlights
   805	
   806	3. **Raycasting Every Mouse Move [Line 414-441]**
   807	   - O(1) to O(N) depending on scene complexity
   808	   - Three.js raycaster is efficient, but intersection testing runs 60 FPS
   809	   - Only 1 raycaster, not per-object
   810	
   811	### Scalability Limits
   812	
   813	- **Shelf Count:** Currently supports 6–12 shelves comfortably (grid layout 2×3 → 4×3)
   814	- **Product Count per Shelf:** Overlay card limited to 290px max width; vertical scroll not implemented
   815	- **Total Products in Inventory Table:** Not tied to 3D scene, separate rendering
   816	
   817	### Optimization Opportunities (Future)
   818	
   819	1. **Defer overlay updates:** Only recalculate positions when camera moves significantly
   820	2. **Virtual scrolling in overlay:** Support >5 products per shelf
   821	3. **Instanced rendering:** Use `THREE.InstancedMesh` for many identical shelves
   822	4. **Level-of-detail:** Simplify shelf geometry for distant shelves
   823	
   824	---
   825	
   826	## 17. TESTING CHECKLIST FOR REGRESSION
   827	
   828	### Visual
   829	- [ ] All shelves render as blue boxes
   830	- [ ] Floor is visible and receives shadows
   831	- [ ] Lighting casts shelf shadows on floor
   832	- [ ] Overlay cards appear above shelves at correct positions
   833	- [ ] Overlay text is readable (no truncation at default zoom)
   834	
   835	### Interaction
   836	- [ ] Mouse hover over shelf highlights emissive glow (darker blue)
   837	- [ ] Mouse hover over shelf displays correct overlay card styling (border, opacity)
   838	- [ ] Click on shelf triggers focus animation (camera moves smoothly)
   839	- [ ] Click on overlay card triggers same focus animation
   840	- [ ] Pan, rotate, zoom controls respond to mouse/touch input
   841	- [ ] Orbit controls respect min/max distance and angle limits
   842	
   843	### Responsiveness
   844	- [ ] Scene resizes correctly on window resize
   845	- [ ] Overlay cards reposition on resize
   846	- [ ] Touch controls work on mobile (one-finger rotate, two-finger zoom)
   847	
   848	### Lifecycle
   849	- [ ] Language change updates overlay text without repositioning
   850	- [ ] Theme change updates background color
   851	- [ ] Navigating away from page cleanly disposes scene (no memory leaks)
   852	- [ ] Re-initializing scene (back to page) works without stale state
   853	
   854	### Edge Cases
   855	- [ ] Empty shelf (no inventory items) shows "Sin productos" / "No products"
   856	- [ ] Shelf name fallback works when name is missing
   857	- [ ] Hover state clears when mouse leaves container
   858	- [ ] Focus animation interrupts if new focus triggered mid-animation
   859	- [ ] Pan limits enforce boundaries (camera doesn't drift off)
   860	
   861	---
   862	
   863	## 18. DEPENDENCIES
   864	
   865	### Three.js Library
   866	```html
   867	<!-- Required in base.html or store_detail.html -->
   868	<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/three.min.js"></script>
   869	<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/examples/js/controls/OrbitControls.js"></script>
   870	```
   871	
   872	### Data Contract (From Flask Template)
   873	- `window.inventoryData` populated with `shelves`, `inventory_by_shelf`, `products_dict`
   874	- Must be set **before** calling `initializeStoreScene()`
   875	
   876	### CSS Dependencies
   877	- CSS custom properties: `--primary`, `--surface`, `--border`, `--text`, `--radius-lg`
   878	- Theme class: `body.dark-theme` toggle support
   879	
   880	### Event System
   881	- Global events: `app:language-changed`, `app:theme-changed`
   882	- Must be triggered from main.js or i18n manager
   883	
   884	---
   885	
   886

commandLine.original:
cd /home/alvaro/xdei/practica_2/FIWARE-app && gh issue create --title "Issue #25: Correcciones visuales menores" --body "## Objetivo
Aplicar correcciones visuales menores en plantillas y representación 3D sin cambiar lógica de negocio.

## Alcance
1. Corregir unidad de capacidad en vista de detalle de Store: m² -> m³ en todos los lugares.
2. Evitar país duplicado en vista Almacenes: mostrar una sola vez bandera + código en la misma celda.
3. Corregir columna de ubicaciones en Productos: mostrar solo número (sin # ni texto adicional en valor).
4. Escena 3D en static/store_3d.js: aumentar tamaño visual de productos y centrar simétricamente distribución en eje Z.
5. Overlay 3D: limitar a 4 productos visibles con scroll vertical (CSS overflow-y:auto + max-height) y evitar propagación de wheel al zoom de OrbitControls usando stopPropagation en el overlay.

## Criterios de aceptación
- Capacidad mostrada en m³ en store_detail.
- País sin duplicidad visual en stores.
- Ubicaciones en products con valor numérico limpio.
- Productos 3D más visibles y distribución centrada/simétrica en Z.
- Overlay con máximo 4 filas visibles y scroll funcional; wheel sobre overlay no hace zoom de escena.

## Restricciones
- No hacer merge ni cerrar issue en esta tarea."

Implementación completada según tu instrucción, sin merge ni cierre del issue.

1. Issue creado en GitHub  
- #25: https://github.com/alvaroprzr/FIWARE-app/issues/25

2. Rama creada  
- feature/issue-25-correcciones-visuales

3. Cambios implementados  
- Corrección de unidad de capacidad m³ en
- Ajuste de celda país para mostrar una sola representación bandera + código en
- Eliminación de texto adicional en columna ubicaciones (solo número) en
- Aumento de tamaño de productos 3D y distribución Z simétrica en
- Overlay 3D con máximo 4 filas visibles + scroll vertical CSS en
- Aislamiento del wheel del overlay con stopPropagation para no propagar a OrbitControls en

4. Commit y push  
- Commit: aad34dece8aa241284693dca603bfb9b8dd72592  
- Mensaje: feat: issue 25 correcciones visuales menores  
- Push remoto: origin/feature/issue-25-correcciones-visuales

5. Estado final  
- Rama actual: feature/issue-25-correcciones-visuales  
- Working tree limpio  
- No se hizo merge ni cierre del issue.
